description: Agilent (Keysight) N9320A spectrum analyzer. Frequency range 9 kHz to 3.0 GHz; resolution bandwidth 10 Hz to 1 MHz. Controlled by SCPI commands over a USB (USBTMC) connection. The N9320B is the direct successor and shares essentially the same SCPI command set.
source: Agilent N9320A Spectrum Analyzer Programmer's Guide (bitsavers.org N9320AProgrammerGuide.pdf) + N9320A User Guide.pdf

identity.description: Standard SCPI identification query; returns manufacturer (Agilent Technologies), model (N9320A), serial number and firmware version.

commands.name: idn
commands.description: Query the instrument identification string (manufacturer, model, serial number, firmware version).

commands.name: rst
commands.description: Reset the analyzer to its factory default (preset) state and clear pending operations.

commands.name: cls
commands.description: Clear the status byte, the standard event status register and the error queue.

commands.name: opc
commands.description: Operation complete query; returns 1 when all pending overlapped operations have finished. Used to synchronise after a sweep.

commands.name: ese
commands.description: Set or query the Standard Event Status Enable register mask.

commands.name: sre
commands.description: Set or query the Service Request Enable register mask.

commands.name: stb
commands.description: Read the Status Byte register.

commands.name: system_preset
commands.description: Restore the analyzer to its preset state (equivalent to the front-panel Preset key).

commands.name: system_error
commands.description: Query and remove the oldest entry from the error queue (returns error number and message).

commands.name: system_version
commands.description: Query the SCPI version to which the analyzer conforms.

commands.name: frequency_center
commands.description: Set or query the center frequency of the analyzer.

commands.name: frequency_span
commands.description: Set or query the frequency span of the analyzer. Setting the span to 0 Hz selects zero-span (time domain) mode.

parameters.name: <span>
parameters.range: 0 Hz (zero span), or 100 Hz to 3.0 GHz (full span). Accepts MIN/MAX/UP/DOWN.

commands.name: frequency_span_full
commands.description: Set the analyzer to the full frequency span (9 kHz to 3.0 GHz).

commands.name: frequency_start
commands.description: Set or query the start frequency of the analyzer (left edge of the display).

commands.name: frequency_stop
commands.description: Set or query the stop frequency of the analyzer (right edge of the display).

commands.name: frequency_center_step
commands.description: Set or query the center frequency step size used by UP/DOWN tuning.

commands.name: frequency_center_step_auto
commands.description: Enable or disable automatic coupling of the center frequency step size, or query its status.

commands.name: power_rlevel
commands.description: Set or query the reference level (the amplitude shown at the top graticule line).

parameters.name: <ampl>
parameters.range: -100 dBm to 30 dBm (depends on the selected amplitude units). Default 0 dBm.

commands.name: power_rlevel_offset
commands.description: Set or query the reference level offset, compensating for gain or loss between the device under test and the analyzer input.

commands.name: power_pdivision
commands.description: Set or query the vertical scale per division (logarithmic amplitude scale).

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.range: 1 dB to 10 dB per division (log scale). Default 10 dB.

commands.name: power_spacing
commands.description: Set or query the vertical scale type (logarithmic or linear).

commands.name: amplitude_units
commands.description: Set or query the amplitude units used for the reference level and marker readouts.

commands.name: power_attenuation
commands.description: Set or query the input RF attenuation.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.range: 0 dB to 50 dB in 5 dB steps. Default coupled (auto).

commands.name: power_attenuation_auto
commands.description: Enable or disable automatic coupling of the input attenuation to the reference level, or query its status.

commands.name: power_gain_state
commands.description: Turn the internal preamplifier (if fitted) on or off, or query its status.

commands.name: bandwidth_resolution
commands.description: Set or query the resolution bandwidth (RBW).

parameters.name: <bandwidth>
parameters.range: 10 Hz to 1 MHz in 1-3-10 sequence (plus a 3 MHz wide-open setting). Accepts MIN/MAX/UP/DOWN.

commands.name: bandwidth_resolution_auto
commands.description: Enable or disable automatic coupling of the resolution bandwidth to the span, or query its status.

commands.name: bandwidth_video
commands.description: Set or query the video bandwidth (VBW).

parameters.name: <bandwidth>
parameters.range: 1 Hz to 1 MHz in 1-3-10 sequence (plus a 3 MHz wide-open setting). Accepts MIN/MAX/UP/DOWN.

commands.name: bandwidth_video_auto
commands.description: Enable or disable automatic coupling of the video bandwidth to the resolution bandwidth, or query its status.

commands.name: bandwidth_video_ratio
commands.description: Set or query the ratio of the video bandwidth to the resolution bandwidth (VBW/RBW) used when video bandwidth is coupled.

commands.name: sweep_time
commands.description: Set or query the sweep time across the displayed frequency span.

parameters.name: <time>
parameters.range: 1 ms to 4000 s (minimum depends on span and RBW). Accepts MIN/MAX/UP/DOWN.

commands.name: sweep_time_auto
commands.description: Enable or disable automatic coupling of the sweep time to span, RBW and VBW, or query its status.

commands.name: sweep_points
commands.description: Set or query the number of sweep (trace) points across the display.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.range: Number of trace points (typically 461). Accepts MIN/MAX.

commands.name: initiate_continuous
commands.description: Set or query the sweep mode: ON for continuous sweeping, OFF for single sweep.

commands.name: initiate_immediate
commands.description: Trigger (initiate) a sweep. In single-sweep mode this starts one sweep; pair with *OPC? or *WAI to wait for completion.

commands.name: initiate_restart
commands.description: Restart the current sweep from the beginning.

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Abort the current sweep or measurement in progress.

commands.name: detector_function
commands.description: Set or query the detector type used to process the captured data of each trace point.

parameters.name: <type>
parameters.range: {NORMal|POSitive|NEGative|SAMPle|AVERage|RMS} (Normal, Pos Peak, Neg Peak, Sample, Average, RMS).

commands.name: average_state
commands.description: Turn trace averaging on or off, or query its status.

commands.name: average_count
commands.description: Set or query the number of sweeps averaged together.

commands.name: average_clear
commands.description: Clear (restart) trace averaging, resetting the accumulated average.

commands.name: trace_mode
commands.description: Set or query the update mode of the specified trace.

commands.name: trace_data
commands.description: Query the amplitude data of the specified trace (returns the comma-separated trace point values).

commands.name: format_data
commands.description: Set or query the data format used when transferring trace data (ASCII or binary).

commands.name: calculate_marker_state
commands.description: Turn the specified marker on or off, or query its status.

commands.name: calculate_marker_mode
commands.description: Set or query the type of the specified marker.

commands.name: calculate_marker_x
commands.description: Set or query the X-axis position (frequency, or time in zero span) of the specified marker.

parameters.name: <freq>
parameters.range: Within the current X-axis range (9 kHz to 3.0 GHz in the frequency domain).

commands.name: calculate_marker_y
commands.description: Query the Y-axis value (amplitude) of the specified marker.

commands.name: calculate_marker_maximum
commands.description: Perform a peak search, placing the specified marker on the maximum amplitude point of the trace.

commands.name: calculate_marker_max_next
commands.description: Place the specified marker on the next peak (next highest amplitude) of the trace.

commands.name: calculate_marker_set_center
commands.description: Set the analyzer center frequency to the frequency of the specified marker (marker-to-center).

commands.name: calculate_marker_set_rlevel
commands.description: Set the analyzer reference level to the amplitude of the specified marker (marker-to-reference-level).

commands.name: calculate_marker_all_off
commands.description: Turn off all the markers.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Set or query the trigger source of the analyzer.
